{{Ambox|color=red|icon=StS2 Map-Boss.png|message=This is a list of map locations in Slay the Spire. See {{2|Map Generation}} for details on how locations are placed and connected.}}

The maps of each {{2|Acts|Act}} in Slay the Spire consist of 7 different types of locations, indicated by different icons.

* [[File:StS2 Map-Monster.png|42px|middle]] Monster Encounter (Normal Enemies)
* [[File:StS2 Map-Elite.png|42px|middle]] Elite Encounter
* [[File:StS2 Map-RestSite.png|42px|middle]] Rest Sites
* [[File:StS2 Map-Event.png|42px|middle]] Unknown
* [[File:StS2 Map-Treasure.png|42px|middle]] Treasure Room
* [[File:StS2 Map-Merchant.png|42px|middle]] Merchant
* [[File:StS2 Map-Boss.png|42px|middle]] Boss Encounter

== Monsters ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Monsters}} for a full list of Monster enemies.}}
Monsters are the basic enemies in Slay the Spire 2. When entering a monster encounter, you will enter battle with one or more enemies.

There are two separate pools of monster encounters in each act: the "easy pool" and the "hard pool". The first three encounters in {{2|Acts|Act 1}}, and the first two encounters in {{2|Acts|Act 2}} and {{2|Acts|Act 3}}, are drawn from the easy pool. All other monster encounters are drawn from the hard pool.

After finishing combat, you will be rewarded with 10-20 ({{Asc|3|8-15|2}}) {{2|Gold}}, a selection of three {{2|Cards}}, and sometimes a {{2|Potions|Potion}}.

== Elites ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Elites}} for a full list of Elite enemies.}}
Elites are much stronger enemies that appear less frequently and will always reward a {{2|Relic}} when defeated. The same elite can appear multiple times in an act, but cannot be encountered twice in a row. 

After finishing combat, you are rewarded with a random {{2|Relic}}, 35-45 ({{Asc|3|26-34|2}}) {{2|Gold}}, sometimes a {{2|Potions|Potion}}, and a selection of 3 cards. Card rewards from elites have a higher chance of yielding rare and uncommon cards (see {{2|Cards#Card Distribution|Card Distribution}}).

Elites are more common above  {{Asc|1|Ascension|2}} and become more powerful on levels {{Asc|8||2}}, and {{Asc|9||2}}.

== Rest Sites ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Rest Sites}} for more info.}}
Rest Sites are map locations that prompt the player to choose one of several options. The default options are Rest (heal for 30% of max HP, rounded down) and Smith ({{2|Mechanics#Upgrade|Upgrade}} a card). More options can be added by obtaining certain Relics. Only one option can be chosen per Rest Site unless you have acquired the {{R|Miniature Tent||2}} relic.

There will always be a Rest Site before the Boss encounter, regardless of the path you take.

== Unknown ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Unknown Location}} for more info.}}
Unknown encounters have a chance to be an [[Slay the Spire 2:Events|Event]], [[Slay the Spire 2:Monsters|Monster]], [[Slay the Spire 2:The Merchant|Merchant]], or [[#Treasure Room|Treasure Room]]. Any time a specific encounter is not seen, its spawn chance increases. When it is seen, it will reset to the original amount. In {{2|Custom Mode}}, the modifier '''Deadly Events''' enables Elites to be able to spawn in Unknown rooms while also increasing the chance to contain a Treasure room. 

== Treasure Room ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Chests}} for more info.}}
Treasure Rooms are guaranteed at the halfway points of Acts 1-3. A single chest will appear where the enemies would be in combat; clicking it opens a reward window. With the {{R|Golden Compass||2}}, there are 2 guaranteed chests towards the middle of the act. Picking up the {{C|Spoils Map||2}} will generate a chest in the next act that contains an extra 600 {{2|Gold}}.

Chests normally contain a single {{2|Relic}} and 42-53 ({{Asc|3|32-40|2}}) {{2|Gold}}. Some {{2|Relics}} such as {{R|Silver Crucible||2}} change what rewards are in chests.

== Merchant ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Merchant}} for more info.}}
The Shop is the primary way to spend {{2|Gold}} in-game. The Merchant sells a selection of 5 character {{2|Cards}}, 2 {{2|Cards#Colorless|Colorless Cards}}, 3 {{2|Relics}}, 3 {{2|Potions}}, and a card removal service that can only be used once per shop.

Some {{2|Relics}} change the prices of the Shop.

== Boss ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Bosses}} for more info.}}
Every Act ends with a Boss encounter. A Rest Site will also be placed on the locations leading to it. Bosses are difficult enemies that have large health pools and strong moves.

Every Act has 3 different bosses that can be fought.

After defeating the Act 1 or Act 2 boss, you are rewarded with 100 ({{Asc|3|75|2}}) {{2|Gold}}, a choice of 3 Rare {{2|Cards}}, and sometimes a random {{2|Potions|Potion}}. The following floor is always a [[#Ancient|Ancient]] floor.

Beating the Act 3 boss will result in a run victory and unlock the next higher {{2|Ascension}}.

== Ancient ==
{{Hatnote|See {{2|Ancients}} for more info.}}
Every Act starts by meeting with an Ancient. Act 1 will always start with {{2|Neow}}. Upon meeting them, Ancients heal the player for 100% ({{Asc|2|80%|2}}) of their missing health. Ancients offer a select choice of unique {{QueryLink|Relics|rarity:Ancient|Ancient Relics|2}}.
